shared_properties: Body footprint stays within the same 32x48 logical bounds as the idle asset.
Eye-glow placement and head crest silhouette remain identical to the base player design.
Color treatment stays limited to black mass, soft teal inner glow, and a tiny exposure-white highlight.
differences: Adds compressed takeoff, airborne stretch, apex tuck, and landing anticipation poses across four frames.
Lower wisps curl downward at the apex to show vertical hang time.
The forward arm reaches slightly upward to separate jump from run motion.

bevy_constraints.bundle_strategy: Reuse the player SpriteSheetBundle and advance the jump atlas by movement state rather than by loop count alone.
bevy_constraints.asset_loading: Load character/player_umbra_jump_4f_12fps.png during Loading and store the atlas handle as the jump state.
runtime_notes: Jump playback should align with the 620 px per second takeoff velocity described in the GDD.
Frame 3 should read as the apex pose and can hold briefly if vertical velocity approaches zero.
Feet and torso anchors must blend cleanly back into idle or run on landing.

context.usage: Short hop state used to cross gaps, clear deck edges, and reposition on lit platforms without losing silhouette readability.

shape.silhouette: Compact jump cycle with crouched takeoff, stretched ascent, tucked apex, and ready-to-land posture.

acceptance_criteria: Sprite sheet is exactly 128x48 px with four 32x48 frames.
Frame sequence clearly reads takeoff, ascent, apex, and landing anticipation without relying on motion blur.
The apex frame keeps the eye glow visible even when the silhouette compresses.
